title: Temporary tunnel
abstract: The invention relates to a temporary tunnel for shielding a road from falling hill side rocks. The tunnel comprises an elongated, open ended sheet metal dome (1) combined with a metal wire mesh (5) covering said dome (1), said wire mesh (5) being designed and dimensioned to catch and hamper larger rocks to penetrate said elongated sheet metal dome (1),said metal dome (1) comprising a plurality of hinged connections (B) pivotally connecting a part of the metal dome (1) so that the part of the elongated sheet metal dome (6a, 6b, 6c) is adapted to be folded. The invention also relates to a method for installing the temporary tunnel on a ground or road to be shielded.
claims:
Claims Patent Claims 1 . A temporary tunnel for shielding a road from falling hill side rocks, characterized in that said tunnel comprises an elongated, open ended sheet metal dome (1 ) combined with a metal wire mesh (5) covering said dome (1 ), said wire mesh (5) being designed and dimensioned to catch and hamper larger rocks to penetrate said elongated sheet metal dome (1 ), said metal dome (1 ) comprising a plurality of hinged connections (B) pivotally connecting a part of the metal dome (1 ) so that the part of the elongated sheet metal dome (6a, 6b , 6c) is adapted to be folded. 2. The temporary tunnel according to claim 1 , characterized in that said sheet metal (4) comprises corrugated plates (P). 3. The temporary tunnel according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that said tunnel is adapted to be prefabricated in a factory and further deployed and erected on site when transported to site . 4. The temporary tunnel according to any of the claims 3, characterized in that said elongated open-ended sheet metal dome (1 ) is unfoldable along a longitudinal top line of said dome (1 ). 5. The temporary tunnel according to any of the claims 3-4, characterized in that said dome (1 ) includes nested and folded elongated dome elements (6a, 6b, 6c), said dome elements (6a, 6b, 6c) being exposed when said dome construction is being unfolded. 6. The temporary tunnel according to any of the claims 5, characterized in that said dome elements (6a, 6b, 6c) comprises three elongated dome elements folded into a triangular cross section internally of and in top of said elongated dome construction (1 ). 8. The temporary tunnel according to any of the claims 5 or 6, characterized in that said three elongated dome elements (6a, 6b, 6c) are hinged together in the longitudinal direction thereof. 9. The temporary tunnel according to any of the claims 1 -8, characterized in that said elongated dome construction is erected by means of powered extendable means (7a, 7b), such as a hydraulic cylinder. 10. The temporary tunnel according to any of the claims 9, characterized in that said powered extendable means (7a, 7b) are adapted to act vertically and horizontally to elevate and unfold the three elongated dome elements (6a, 6b, 6c) and to widen out the road contacting parts (3a - 3j) of the dome construction in order to widely open the tunnel cross section 1 1 . The temporary tunnel according to claim 10, characterized in that hinged connections (B) having locking means to lock the dome elements in position. 1 1 . A method for installing a temporary tunnel according to any one of the claims 1 - 10, characterized in that the method comprising the following steps c) Transporting the temporary tunnel in a folded position to the ground or road to be shielded, by retracting a part (6a, 6b, 6c) of the metal sheet dome into the inside of the tunnel, d) Unfolding the temporary tunnel at the ground or road to be shielded to an unfolded position where the entire metal sheet dome (1 ) is exposed 

 through the entire periphery (3, 6a, 6b, 6c, 3) of the metal sheet dome (1 ).
